definition: The FX price for the standard settlement horizon (often T+2 for major pairs); contrasts with forward rates.
The first currency in a pair; the amount of quote currency needed to buy one unit of base.
The second currency in a pair; priced per one unit of base currency.
Difference between dealer sell (ask) and buy (bid) prices; liquidity tends to tighten spreads.
Smallest conventional price increment for a pair (often 0.0001 for majors like EUR/USD).
An exchange rate between two non-USD currencies, often derived from USD legs.
European Central Bank; policy and reference rates influence EUR crosses.
Public HTTP service that exposes ECB-based daily FX rates for teaching and examples.
